Functional Modules.Function Description: Drives a piezo buzzer via PWM for audible telemetry and recovery beacons.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Signal-level MOSFET gate, piezo buzzer terminals.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: Signal-level MOSFET (low-side) switches buzzer ground. PWM from MCU modulates buzzer activation for variable tones/patterns. Safety pull-down resistor on MOSFET gate ensures OFF state during power-up/MCU init.
Functional Modules.Signal Chain: The microcontroller (STM32F405RGT6) generates a PWM signal from one of its timer output channels. This signal is connected to the gate of a low-side N-channel MOSFET (AO3400A) through a current-limiting resistor (not in library, assumed external). A pull-down resistor (not in library, assumed external) is connected from the MOSFET gate to ground to ensure it remains off during MCU initialization or when the PWM pin is high-impedance. The MOSFET's drain is connected to the negative terminal of the piezo buzzer, while the buzzer's positive terminal is connected to a power supply (e.g., 5V from USB Type-C connector's VBUS via a regulator if needed). The MOSFET's source is connected to ground. When the PWM signal is high, the MOSFET turns on, completing the circuit and allowing current to flow through the buzzer, producing sound. The PWM duty cycle and frequency control the tone and volume.

Recommended Components.Recommendation Reason: Provides a flexible, programmable PWM output to drive the MOSFET gate for precise control of buzzer tones and patterns.
Acts as a voltage-controlled switch to ground the piezo buzzer, enabling PWM modulation for sound generation.
Provides 5V VBUS power from USB for the system, which can be used directly or regulated for the buzzer and MCU.
Steps down the 5V USB power to a stable 3.3V required by the microcontroller and other digital components.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Low-side switching stage that regulates high-voltage battery bus connection to an external initiator for deployment. Includes integrated continuity-sensing for real-time monitoring.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Microcontroller control signal (PWM or digital), high-voltage battery bus, initiator terminal voltage.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Power MOSFET gate, microcontroller feedback input (continuity signal), external initiator load.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: Power MOSFET (low-side) switches initiator ground path. Voltage-divider network across initiator terminals generates a scaled low-voltage feedback signal indicating continuity/open-circuit. Safety pull-down resistor on MOSFET gate ensures OFF state during power-up/MCU init.
Functional Modules.Signal Chain: The microcontroller (STM32F405RGT6) generates a PWM or digital control signal to drive the gate of a low-side N-channel MOSFET (AO3400A). The MOSFET's drain connects to the initiator's ground terminal, and its source connects to system ground. When the MOSFET is turned on, it completes the circuit from the high-voltage battery bus (connected to the initiator's positive terminal) through the initiator to ground, allowing high current to flow for deployment. A voltage-divider network is placed across the initiator terminals (between the high-voltage bus and the MOSFET's drain) to generate a scaled low-voltage feedback signal. This feedback signal is connected to an ADC input on the microcontroller for real-time continuity monitoring (detecting open-circuit or proper connection). A pull-down resistor is connected between the MOSFET's gate and ground to ensure it remains off during power-up or microcontroller initialization. The system may require a regulated 3.3V supply for the microcontroller and logic, which could be derived from the high-voltage bus via a switching regulator (TPS565208) or an LDO (AMS1117), but the component library lacks resistors, capacitors, and other passives needed for the voltage divider, gate driving, and power supply filtering.

Recommended Components.Functional Position: Microcontroller for generating control signal and reading continuity feedback
Recommended Components.Recommendation Reason: Provides PWM/digital output for MOSFET gate control and has ADC inputs for reading the scaled continuity feedback voltage.

Recommended Components.Functional Position: Low-side power switch for initiator ground path
Recommended Components.Recommendation Reason: N-channel MOSFET suitable for low-side switching with high current capability (5.7A continuous) and low on-resistance.

Recommended Components.Functional Position: Step-down switching regulator to convert high-voltage battery bus to 3.3V for microcontroller and logic
Recommended Components.Recommendation Reason: Can handle input voltages up to 17V (suitable for a high-voltage battery bus) and provide 5A output, sufficient for powering the microcontroller and other logic. More efficient than an LDO for high input-to-output voltage differences.

Functional Modules.Function Description: Provides control signals, reads feedback, and ensures safe default states for both deployment and indicator subsystems.
Functional Modules.Signal Sources: Continuity feedback voltage (from deployment channel), system power-good signals.
Functional Modules.Signal Destinations: Deployment channel control signal, indicator driver PWM signal.
Functional Modules.Implementation Logic: MCU outputs digital/PWM signals to control MOSFETs. Reads analog continuity voltage via ADC. Internal firmware ensures signals are only asserted after system initialization and safety checks. Pull-down resistors on all control GPIOs provide hardware safety.
Functional Modules.Signal Chain: The module's core is the STM32F405RGT6 microcontroller. It receives analog continuity feedback voltage from the deployment channel via an ADC input pin (e.g., PC0). It reads digital system power-good signals via GPIO pins. After internal firmware initialization and safety checks, it generates a deployment channel control signal as a digital output (e.g., PA4) to drive an N-channel MOSFET (AO3400A) gate. The MOSFET switches the high-side deployment load. The MCU also generates a PWM signal (e.g., PA6) to drive an indicator LED or similar load, likely through another MOSFET. Hardware safety is ensured by external pull-down resistors on all control GPIOs (PA4, PA6) to guarantee default-off states. The AMS1117 provides a stable 3.3V supply for the MCU and logic from a higher input voltage (e.g., 5V). The TPS565208 could provide a higher current or different voltage rail if needed for peripherals. The W25Q128JV stores firmware and data via SPI. The USB Type-C connector with USBLC6 protection provides power input and a programming/debug interface via UART or USB.

Recommended Components.Recommendation Reason: Provides the necessary GPIOs for control and feedback, ADC for analog continuity reading, PWM generation for indicator control, and sufficient processing power for safety logic and system management.
N-channel MOSFET suitable for low-side switching of the deployment load, controlled directly by the MCU's GPIO with appropriate gate driving.
Provides a stable 3.3V supply from a higher input voltage (e.g., 5V USB) for the MCU and other digital logic, ensuring reliable operation.
Provides non-volatile storage for firmware, configuration parameters, and data logging via a high-speed SPI interface.
Provides a standardized, reversible connector for 5V power input and a potential UART-based programming/debug interface via the D+/D- lines.
Protects the sensitive USB data lines (D+/D-) from electrostatic discharge and electrical transients, ensuring interface reliability.
